subject: [PATCH v5 05/36] mm: add ZONELIST_PRIVATE(_NOFALLBACK) for N_MEMORY_PRIVATE nodes.

2026-07-20.patch_summary: This patch adds a new zonelist type, ZONELIST_PRIVATE(_NOFALLBACK), to isolate N_MEMORY_PRIVATE nodes from unintentional allocations. It introduces a new alloc_flags flag, ALLOC_ZONELIST_PRIVATE, and modifies the page allocator to use this new zonelist when allocating pages from private nodes. The patch also adds explicit allocation functions for private nodes to prevent open-coding. This change makes it impossible for normal callers of page_alloc to reach N_MEMORY_PRIVATE nodes unless explicitly called for.

reviews.author: Richard Cheng
reviews.summary: Reviewer Richard Cheng questioned whether ZONELIST_PRIVATE should use an explicit target nodemask to prevent fallback to another owner's private node, potentially violating node isolation.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Gregory,

A question about this part, if my understanding is right,
ZONELIST_PRIVATE contains all private nodes, should we use some explicit target
nodemask here to make sure only the targeting owner's private node can be touched?

Otherwise it might fall back into a different owner's private node.

--Richard

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges that private nodes cannot fall back to other private nodes in the current design and suggests waiting for a specific use case before extending zonelist construction.

2026-07-22.patch_summary: This patch adds a new zonelist isolation mechanism to prevent unintentional allocations from reaching N_MEMORY_PRIVATE nodes. It introduces two new zonelists: ZONELIST_PRIVATE and ZONELIST_PRIVATE_NOFALLBACK, which are used for private node allocations. The patch also adds explicit alloc() functions for private node allocations and modifies the allocator's behavior to use these new zonelists. This change aims to make N_MEMORY_PRIVATE nodes effectively invisible to normal callers of page_alloc, while still allowing intentional private node allocations.
